# Transcrição: 01 How to Set Up Third Party Reporting

- **Fonte:** 01 How to Set Up Third Party Reporting
- **Duração:** 04:45
- **Idioma:** en
- **Modelo:** small

---

## [00:00 - 03:00]

So what I want to do now is I want to make sure that the communication lines with the client are kept really, really open. Now, it's a lot of work to manually go in every single day and export a report for clients. And in fact, I would discourage you from sending clients a daily report. All it does is stress them out and they look at the daily numbers when really they should be looking at weekly stats. But what we want to do is have a nice report that gets sent to them once a week. We also want to communicate to them. So that's the account manager's role that this will be the case. This is what's going to happen. And because we don't want to show them our ad tracking sheet, which is not the prettiest thing. And also there's a lot of stats in there that are not relevant to them. So typically for e-commerce, we're using Suido for this. What you're going to do is click on create a report. We're going to name it the client's report. All right. We're going to collect the data source. Okay. So this is what it's going to do. It's going to populate all of this, which is again too intense. So what we want to do is we want to edit this so that it only shows them what they really need to see. So you're going to click on edit. You can add your logos, by the way, and make it really pretty. So what clients normally want to see with... We're just going to delete all of these and we're going to basically rebuild it. So really the four metrics that an e-commerce client is interested in is, what did we spend over the week? Well, how many purchases did we get? What was the cost per purchase? And what's the ROA on that? Now, they also want to hear recommendations, right? Especially if it was a bad week. What are you doing as an agency? That's something that you're going to have to send separately. So that's the account manager's role to communicate that with the team and then back to the client. All right. So let's start off with our purchases. We're going to add our first KPI. We're going to click Facebook ads, single metric. And basically what we want to do here is we'll just name this purchases. And then under metrics, we're going to click on purchase. Save this. We also want to have cost per purchase, right? So we're going to repeat this process here. Cost per purchase. Go under metrics and click purchase. Cost per purchase.

## [03:00 - 04:45]

There it is. Now, if you're using Shopify, these are the basically the events that you're going to be using, right? The other one that they want to know is ROA. So we're going to go in here again. Okay. We're going to click settings. And then we just need to add the spend. And we're going to click on cost. Okay. So we can organize it like this. And obviously you can save the header. Okay. And now it's pulled up the stats. So it's pretty basic. And this is really all they need to see. So you can make it look pretty. Feel free to edit this, et cetera. Okay. So the last step is we just want to schedule a report. So this gets sent to the client every single week. So we're going to click week here. And now it'll send out every Monday. And then we can pose the message, make it from the account manager and then send it to the client. So you have a nice little line here's your overview for the week from the previous week of your Facebook ads. And you can set that up. And then the client will automatically get an email on Monday every week with just a really clear, clean overview. Now as an account manager, they should be asking the client, especially in the early stages of the relationship, are there any other stats that you would like to see here or that are important to you? They should just clarify this is what we start off with with clients so that it's not overwhelming. These are the most ultimately these are the most important stats for a client, right? How much did I spend? What's the cost to me for a sale? How many sales did I get? And what's my ROA? However, some clients do want to see other things. So ask the client, keep those communication lines open. And this is basically how to set up the reporting.
